metadata.region: Southcentral Alaska — Cook Inlet, Kachemak Bay, Kenai Peninsula
metadata.disclaimer: This is a reference guide. Emergency orders can change these limits at any time. Always verify with ADFG before fishing.

pacificHalibut.sizeRestriction: Second fish must be 32 inches or under (total length)
pacificHalibut.annualLimitNonResident: Subject to IPHC annual limits — verify annually
pacificHalibut.notes: Guided (charter) anglers may have additional restrictions. IPHC regulations apply in addition to state rules.

kingSalmon.minimumSize: 28 inches (fish under 28 inches are jacks, may not be retained in some areas)
kingSalmon.season: Year-round, but productive fishing May through July
kingSalmon.notes: Annual limits for non-residents vary by specific management area. Some areas require immediate recording of harvest on the back of the fishing license.

silverSalmon.notes: Bag limit may be increased by emergency order in years of strong returns.

sockeyeSalmon.notes: Limit may be 3 or 6 depending on area and run strength. Check area-specific regulations.

pinkSalmon.notes: Pink salmon run in distinct odd-year cycles in Southcentral Alaska.

rockfish.yelloweyeSubLimit: May be restricted — check current regulations
rockfish.descendingDeviceRequired: Recommended for all non-pelagic rockfish released from depth over 100 feet
rockfish.notes: Non-pelagic rockfish are extremely long-lived and slow to reproduce. Conservation is critical. Prefer targeting pelagic species.

lingcod.notes: Season dates protect spawning fish. Verify current dates annually.

earlyRun.bagLimit: Subject to frequent emergency order closures
earlyRun.notes: Early run has been heavily restricted or closed in recent years due to weak returns. Verify EO status.

lateRun.annualLimitNonResident: Typically 1-2 from the Kenai (set by EO)
lateRun.gearRestrictions: Single hook only in some sections
lateRun.notes: The premier king salmon fishery. Extremely competitive and heavily regulated.

sockeyeSalmon.bagLimit: 3-6 per day depending on run strength and EO
sockeyeSalmon.season: July through August, specific dates set annually

russianRiver.separatePermit: Russian River/Kenai River sockeye permit may be required

silverSalmon.season: July through October, varies by section
silverSalmon.notes: September and October offer the best fishing with fewer crowds.

rainbowTrout.seasonClosures: Some sections closed April through June for spawning
rainbowTrout.baitRestrictions: Artificial lures only in most sections
rainbowTrout.maximumRetention: No retention of rainbow trout over 16 inches in most sections

nickDudiakFishingLagoon.type: Stocked tidal lagoon on Homer Spit
nickDudiakFishingLagoon.separateRegulations: May differ from general Cook Inlet rules

kenaiRiver.season: Typically July, specific dates announced annually
